title: strncpy used for source_name copies - potential truncation without warning
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/app_interface/hal_bridge.c:234-236, 273-278

  strncpy(app->source_name, hal->source_name,
      sizeof(app->source_name) - 1U);
  app->source_name[sizeof(app->source_name) - 1U] = '\0';

And:
  strncpy(app->source_name, hal->source_name,
      sizeof(app->source_name) - 1U);
  app->source_name[sizeof(app->source_name) - 1U] = '\0';
  strncpy(app->rejection_source_name, hal->rejection_source_name,
      sizeof(app->rejection_source_name) - 1U);
  app->rejection_source_name[sizeof(app->rejection_source_name) - 1U] = '\0';

The code correctly null-terminates after strncpy, preventing buffer overflow. However:

1. If source strings exceed 23 characters, they are silently truncated
2. No warning or error is raised
3. Truncated names may cause identification issues downstream

For a source_name[24] buffer:
- Input: 'my-long-location-source-name-v2' (31 chars)
- Output: 'my-long-location-source' (23 chars) - truncated

This is not a memory safety bug but may cause operational issues. Consider:
1. Logging when truncation occurs
2. Returning -EINVAL if name exceeds capacity
3. Documenting the 23-character limit
close_reason: Added strnlen checks before strncpy in hal_bridge.c from_hal functions; returns -ENAMETOOLONG on truncation.
